properties.displayName: REPLACE — Rule name including technique ID e.g. Suspicious PowerShell Execution (T1059.001)
properties.description: REPLACE — 2-3 sentences: (1) what this detects, (2) what the attacker is doing, (3) triage guidance. Write it so an analyst understands what fired and what to check first.
properties.severity: REPLACE — one of: Informational | Low | Medium | High | Critical
properties.query: REPLACE — paste your KQL here. Escape newlines as \n and backslashes as \\ before pasting into JSON. Test in Sentinel Logs first. Column names here must match entityMappings below.

fieldMappings.columnName: REPLACE — must match a column returned by your query e.g. Computer

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: REPLACE — Dynamic title using {{ColumnName}} syntax e.g. Suspicious service on {{Computer}}
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: REPLACE — Dynamic body e.g. Service {{ServiceName}} created with path {{ServiceFileName}}
